title: Starmer Watch — Pressure Index
description: Composite pressure index, top news, and market signals for Keir Starmer's leadership.

items.title: Pressure Index 62/100 (elevated)
items.content_text: Public pressure bloc is above the modeled 81 MP trigger. Pressure Index 62/100 (elevated). Polymarket's top Starmer-exit signal is 17.5%.

items.title: Guardian (Open Platform): Armed forces minister Al Carns quits over defence spending plan – UK politics live
items.content_text: Armed forces minister Al Carns quits over defence spending plan – UK politics live

items.title:
Bluesky · @georgeeaton.bsky.social: Starmer’s post-election speech gets worse with age. 

He said “incremental change won’t cut it” on defence, growth and Europe and has offered… exactly that.
items.content_text:
Starmer’s post-election speech gets worse with age. 

He said “incremental change won’t cut it” on defence, growth and Europe and has offered… exactly that.

items.title: Bluesky · @stephenkb.bsky.social: Call me cynical, but nothing says “I was waiting to see if I was offered defence secretary” like waiting the best part of six hours after your boss has quit to resign.

items.title: Bluesky · @stephenkb.bsky.social: I disagree only in that I think it will probably be one of the disasters where Starmer himself had the right instincts from the start that ends up being the definitive case study.
